project_reference.core_research_question: Is complexification forced by thermodynamic necessity -- self-modelers requiring free energy, entropy gradients, time-orientation, chirality?
project_reference.current_focus: v7.0 Arrow of Time, Complexification, and Evolutionary Selection

scope.question: Can the experiential measure framework on structure space be formalized with rigorous proofs — specifically, can Theorem A (BB negligibility) be assembled from its constituent lemmas, can the experiential density rho's Lipschitz stability be proven, and does the Born-Fisher-Experiential conjecture survive a qubit falsification test?
in_scope: Assemble Theorem A proof from BEGK, Freidlin-Wentzell, Donsker-Varadhan, and QSD lemmas with explicit error scaling
Prove Lipschitz stability of rho(P) under kernel perturbations
Compute I_vN(B;M) and S_vN(B) for toy qubit model to test Born-Fisher conjecture
out_of_scope: Countable state space generalization (Stage 1 of extension program)
Full variational structure of mu_Q beyond qubit test
Reference measure nu on structure space
Eternal inflation regime (fixed epsilon, T -> infinity)
unresolved_questions: Exact form of L(|Omega|, spectral_gap) in Lipschitz bound
Whether qubit model needs 2x2 or larger Hilbert space to be informative

user_asserted_anchors: Toy model rho = 0.347 for self-modeling observer
Parametric peak at tracking accuracy alpha ~= 0.498 with I*/H = 0.507
Three-state chain verification matches analytical formula within 1% error
known_good_baselines: Seven 16-state Markov chain toy model results from composite_self_model.py
Three-state Freidlin-Wentzell metastability verification from three_state_chain.py
context_gaps: Explicit constants in BEGK lemma composition not yet worked through
Qubit model Hilbert space dimension not yet decided
crucial_inputs: Theorem A 7-lemma dependency graph from draft Section 8
Experiential density definition rho(p) = I(B;M) * (1 - I(B;M)/H(B))

formulations: Finite irreducible continuous-time Markov chains with reversible Metropolis-type dynamics
Product space Omega = B x M with factorization condition on transition kernel
allowed_estimator_families: direct proof composition from established lemmas
forbidden_estimator_families: proof by analogy without explicit bounds
stop_and_rethink_conditions: BEGK/DV/QSD error bounds do not compose — exponential suppression breaks in the full chain
I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 fails for Born-rule distributions in qubit model

observables.name: Theorem A proof completeness
observables.definition: Self-contained proof assembling BEGK, FW, DV, QSD lemmas showing mu_BB/mu_stable <= C*exp(-(Delta_s - Delta_b - alpha)/epsilon)

observables.name: Lipschitz constant characterization
observables.definition: Proven bound |rho(P) - rho(P')| <= L*||P-P'||_inf with L depending on |Omega| and spectral gap

observables.name: Qubit Born-Fisher test result
observables.definition: Numerical value of I_vN(B;M)/S_vN(B) for Born-rule distributions in toy qubit composite process

claims.statement: Theorem A can be assembled as a complete proof from established metastability lemmas with explicit error scaling
The experiential density rho is Lipschitz continuous in the transition kernel with characterizable constant
Born-rule distributions satisfy or violate I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 in the toy qubit model

deliverables.description: Self-contained proof of Theorem A with 7 lemmas composed, explicit error scaling, constants deferred to appendix
Proof of Lipschitz stability of rho with L characterized in terms of |Omega| and spectral gap
Python implementation of toy qubit composite process computing I_vN(B;M) and S_vN(B) for Born-rule and non-Born distributions

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-theorem-a
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify each lemma cites the specific theorem from the source literature, verify the composition chain has no gaps, verify the final bound has the claimed exponential form
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Complete logical chain from lemma statements to Theorem A conclusion with no deferred steps in the main argument

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-lipschitz
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify perturbation theory for stationary distributions is applied correctly, verify MI continuity bound is tight, verify L dependence is explicit
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Rigorous bound with no hand-waving steps; numerical verification against toy model perturbations

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-born-fisher
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run qubit model code, compute I_vN(B;M)/S_vN(B) for Born-rule distribution, compare to 0.5
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Clear numerical answer: ratio equals 0.5 (within numerical precision) or demonstrably does not

references.locator: Bovier, Eckhoff, Gayrard, Klein — Metastability papers (2001-2004)
references.why_it_matters: Provides exponential residence time bounds used in Theorem A Lemma 2

references.locator: Freidlin, Wentzell — Random Perturbations of Dynamical Systems (1984/2012)
references.why_it_matters: Provides cycle hierarchy and communication heights for basin decomposition in Theorem A Lemma 1

references.locator: Donsker, Varadhan — Large deviations for Markov processes (1975-1983)
references.why_it_matters: Provides concentration inequality for weighted empirical measure in Theorem A Lemma 6

references.locator: Baez, Dolan — From Finite Sets to Feynman Diagrams (2001)
references.why_it_matters: Defines groupoid cardinality that motivates the 1/|Aut(x)| weighting on structure space

references.locator: Lopez-Ruiz, Mancini, Calbet — A statistical measure of complexity (1995)
references.why_it_matters: The rho density belongs to this family — establishes the pedigree of the I*(1-I/H) functional form
Places rho in the broader family of measures that peak between order and randomness

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-theorem-a
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Another proof sketch with 'the tools exist' language but no assembled composition
forbidden_proxies.reason: This is exactly what the existing draft already has — repeating it is not progress

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-lipschitz
forbidden_proxies.proxy: More toy model numerical runs confirming intuition without formal Lipschitz bound
forbidden_proxies.reason: Numerical evidence for rho stability already exists in the parametric sweep; the gap is the proof

weakest_anchors: BEGK error bounds may not compose cleanly across all 7 lemmas — the individual bounds are established but their product has not been verified to preserve the exponential form
The qubit model dimension choice may be too small to be informative about the general conjecture
unvalidated_assumptions: Reversibility assumption in Theorem A (real observers are non-equilibrium)
Factorization condition exactness (real systems have approximate factorization)
competing_explanations: Alternative density functions in the LMC family with same boundary conditions but different peak behavior could work equally well
disconfirming_observations: BEGK/DV/QSD error bounds do not compose — exponential suppression of mu_BB/mu_stable breaks in the assembled proof
Born-rule distributions do NOT satisfy I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 in the qubit model

**Route 1 NEGATIVE**: Effros-Stormer conditional expectations do not force V_{1/2} complexification; Peirce interface V_1.V_{1/2} is trivially scalar (eigenvalue 1/2); no C*-subalgebra inside h_3(O) (Shirshov-Cohn) (HIGH)

**Route 2 NEGATIVE**: State-effect duality gives only real inner product; P_1(v*w) = [Re(x3 bar(y3)) + Re(x2 bar(y2))] E_11 is real scalar; compatible complex structures form 56-dim family O(16)/U(8), none selected (HIGH)
**Route 3 NEGATIVE**: GNS construction fails -- three independent obstructions: h_3(O) exceptional (GNS gives R^27 not C^k), dim 27 odd (no complex structure), V_1=R bottleneck (scalar action) (HIGH)

**Route 4 WEAK POSITIVE**: A tensor_R V_{1/2} = A tensor_C V_{1/2}^C canonically, but generic algebraic tautology (works for any real V), not h_3(O)-specific; equivalent to Phase 18 extension-of-scalars (HIGH)

**Phase 22 synthesis**: All 4 routes converge on same obstruction -- V_1 = R*E_11 is 1-dimensional, Peirce action is scalar multiplication, no complex structure transmittable through this interface (HIGH)
**Peirce projections**: P_1 = 2L_e^2 - L_e, P_{1/2} = -4L_e^2 + 4L_e, P_0 = 2L_e^2 - 3L_e + 1 (Lagrange interpolation) (HIGH)

**Internal/spacetime chirality distinction**: omega_6 (Euclidean Cl(6)) vs Gamma_5 (Lorentzian Cl(d-1,1)); full SM chirality needs both (HIGH)
**Lattice framing hierarchy (VALD-01)**: framing => spin => orientability; Paper 6 lattice provides time-orientation + space-orientation + spin structure (HIGH)
**Three-consequence theorem (CHIR-02)**: single u in S^6 determines (a) gauge group [constructive], (b) chirality [constructive], (c) time-orientation requirement [constraint] (HIGH)
**Updated 9-link chain**: L7 annotated with cross-paper dependency to Paper 6 time-orientation (HIGH)
**Assumption register A1-A6**: A1-A4 from Paper 7, A5 continuum limit, A6 Lorentzian signature (HIGH)
**Phase 24 verification**: 5/5 contract targets, 12/12 physics checks, confidence HIGH (HIGH)
open_questions: RESOLVED: Sign of J (AFM vs FM) -- both analyzed; WVCH and channel capacity bounds are sign-independent
RESOLVED: Does self-modeling locality force area-law entanglement? -- YES via WVCH (thermal MI) and channel capacity (pure S)
RESOLVED: Does a lattice of self-modeling M_n(C)^sa systems satisfy Jacobson's thermodynamic inputs? -- YES: (J1) area law established, (J2) entanglement first law exact, (J3) MVEH assumed as A5
RESOLVED: What is the precise gap? -- Assumption A1 (thermal state) for WVCH, Assumption A2 (pure state) for channel capacity
RESOLVED: Can Jacobson's entanglement equilibrium be formulated on a finite lattice? -- Formulated in continuum limit (Wilsonian), not on finite lattice directly
Basis-independence -> diagonal U(n) invariance of h_xy is a physical postulate, not a theorem [NOTE for Paper 6]
Can Assumption A2 (pure global state) be derived from self-modeling axioms? [HIGH, affects synthesis]
RESOLVED: "Which state?" -- resolved via three perspectives (thermal MI, pure S, delta S)
RESOLVED: Does self-modeling lattice satisfy Jacobson's MVEH? -- Assumed as A5 with MaxEnt motivation; not derived. Main gap for Phase 10.
RESOLVED: Can modular Hamiltonian locality (A3) be verified numerically on small lattices? -- YES, SRF=0.9993 for Heisenberg AFM at N=16

decisions.summary: Composite V_BM defined as MINIMAL OUS with product-form SP
Correlation form B(a,b) = tau(a * phi^{-1}(b)) bridges faithful tracking to local tomography
Entangled sector eliminated via minimality + SP closure + non-degeneracy
Researcher approved -- load-bearing: non-degeneracy -> span d_B*d_M -> equals maximal for complex only
S4 proved via facial orthogonality -- phi-independent, applies to all f with f(0,x)=0
All S1-S7 established -- vdW Theorem 1 invoked for EJA classification
Corrected product uses sqrt Peirce 1-space feedback; faithful self-modeling selects maximal f, recovering Luders
Decisive insight was Peirce 1-space resolution (S3), not S4
E(B) framing selected over E(B x M)
Sequential product route chosen over direct involution construction
Started milestone v3.0: GR Extension
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle -- extending self-modeling to derive GR via locality -> area law -> Jacobson

decisions.summary: Diagonal U(n) invariance (not U(n) x U(n)) is the correct constraint on h_xy
h_xy = alpha*1 + JF (SWAP) forced by Schur-Weyl duality for S_2
Graph topology G = (V, E) is input (background dependence acknowledged)
Sign of J undetermined by SP constraints; affects Phase 9 area-law analysis
Self-modeling h_xy = JF identical to Heisenberg; v_LR independent of n
WVCH (not Hastings) as primary area-law theorem -- gap fails for both signs of J
Thermal state identification (Assumption A1) explicitly flagged as gap
Both signs of J analyzed independently; WVCH bound is sign-independent
Channel capacity + DPI for pure-state S area law; Assumption A2 (pure state) flagged as gap
delta S (not S) is what Jacobson needs -- decouples area-law from state-selection
Modular Hamiltonian locality (A3) is weakest anchor; physically motivated but unproven
MVEH identified as main Phase 10 gap, separate from area-law question
Wilsonian continuum limit framed as physical argument, not rigorous construction
MVEH stated as Assumption A5 (not derived); MaxEnt motivation; gap identifies what self-modeling property would establish it
Conformal restriction stated honestly; 1D AFM Heisenberg identified as most controlled case (exact CFT)
Adopted Jacobson 2016 G = 1/(4 eta) for d=3; independent derivation confirmed tensor structure and sign
Lambda explicitly left undetermined; no prediction claimed
Gap statement as first-class component, not footnote
Honest Paper 6 framing: UV completion for Jacobson, not derivation of GR from self-modeling alone
CCM 2017 spatial constraint consistent with full spacetime Einstein equation
Vectorized bit-manipulation for Hamiltonian construction (not Kronecker products)
FM test uses product state |all-up> (eigsh degenerate superposition has nonzero S)
TFI c threshold relaxed to 0.08 (Affleck-Ludwig OBC corrections at N=16)
6 non-rectangular subregions added for boundary diversity on 4x4 PBC lattice
Honest assessment: 1D AFM Heisenberg is gapless c=1 CFT with log scaling, not strict area law
SU(2) singlet rho_A invariance under single-site unitaries necessitated Hamiltonian perturbation for MVEH
Interaction range metric for K_A locality (max pairwise distance of non-I Pauli sites)
MVEH framed as definitional via Connes-Rovelli thermal time hypothesis, not Assumption A5
Derivation chain table (Table I) placed in introduction main text as structural spine
Two gaps: continuum limit (standard, shared) and conformal approximation (minor). MVEH no longer a gap.
Adopted Jacobson 2016 G = 1/(4 eta) in paper; MVEH dissolution uses exact phrasing from researcher lock
Conformal approximation stated as honest caveat with Speranza 2016 reference
All numerical results framed as "consistent with" / "supporting evidence", never "proves"
2D R^2=0.885 shortfall honestly noted with PBC wrapping explanation
Started milestone v4.0: Spectral Triple from Self-Modeling
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle -- spectral triple from self-modeling composite, targeting Standard Model via Connes classification

decisions.summary: Naive algebra action pi(a) = diag(a tensor I, a tensor I) used as primary; contragredient also satisfies order zero
pi_o(b) = diag(I_n tensor b^T, I_n tensor b^T) derived via J antilinearity chain
[gamma, pi(a)] = 0 fails for ALL non-scalar a -- only C*I satisfies even condition with SWAP-based chirality
Three resolution paths: (a) different algebra action, (b) different gamma, (c) odd spectral triple
Per-sector n^2 = k^2 is the correct CCM comparison (not naive 2n^2 = k^2); factor of 2 is J-doubling
Barrett spinor space V = C^2; H = V tensor M_n(C) with two sectors
A_F identification deferred to Phase 15; no premature SM claims
D moduli space dim = n^2(n^2+1) at general n; involution constraint M = J_+ M^T J_+
n=1 moduli dim is 2 (not 1): J constraint trivially satisfied since J_+ = I at n=1
Barrett cross-check is partial -- Barrett's D includes first-order condition not yet imposed
Sub-block constraints: M_{12} = M_{12}^T, M_{21} = M_{21}^T, M_{22} = -M_{11}^T
Barrett J constraint requires K^T = K (symmetric), NOT K = scalar
Barrett D_1(X) = KX + XK = 2(K*X) is twice the Jordan product = linearized sequential product
KO-dim 6 epsilon'=+1 selects L_K + R_K (anticommutator) over L_K - R_K (commutator)
Barrett subspace dim = n(n+1)/2 (10 at n=4); expected to match first-order condition subspace
A_F = M_n(C) for Barrett-form D -- first-order condition is automatically satisfied for all a, b
The proof reduces to [L_C, R_b] = 0 (associativity), same identity as order zero
Gauge group U(n) at n=4 gives U(4), not SM -- structural consequence of simple algebra
CCM difference is structural: simple M_n(C) vs direct sum M_2(H) + M_4(C)
A_F = C (dim 1) for generic D from full moduli space; only Barrett D gives A_F = M_n(C)
No intermediate subalgebra exists for simple M_n(C): only C or M_n(C)
Medium success tier: valid spectral triple with U(n) gauge group, but SM requires direct sum algebra
Master formula [[M, L_a], R_b](X) = sum_k [A_k, a] X [b, B_k] -- Barrett is pure-left type
Started milestone v5.0: Chirality from h_3(O) via Cl(6)
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle -- h_3(O)/Cl(6) route to chirality replaces spectral triple approach

decisions.summary: S_{10}^+ convention following Boyle 2020; chirality deferred to Phase 19
Rank-1 idempotent e = E_11 taken as given input (Gap B step 1)
Backtracking trigger NOT fired -- complexification follows from C*-nature alone
U(1) charges (-4, 2, -1) recorded but physical meaning (B-L) deferred to Phase 19-20
Local-to-global complexification noted as consistent completion, not formally necessary
u = e_7 (Fano) as complex structure; Cl(10) via gamma_k x I + i*omega_6 x tau_a; P selects omega_6 = -i eigenspace (Todorov particle convention)
Witt basis a_j = (1/2)(gamma_{2j-1} + i*gamma_{2j}); Furey octonion-label remapping deferred to Plan 02
omega_6 separates chiralities before Pati-Salam breaking; LEFT not diagonal embedding
Schwinger boson SU(2) from Cl(4) Witt: J3L = (m1+m2-1)/2, J3R = (m1-m2)/2
B-L = 1 - (2/3)N for Pati-Salam assignment (N=1 quarks: +1/3, N=3 leptons: -1)
Pati-Salam left-right symmetric convention: 16 contains L+R particles, same Q operator for both
P selects omega_6 = +i eigenspace = odd N (N=1 quarks + N=3 leptons)
[SU(3)xSU(3)]/Z_3 is maximal-rank subgroup of F_4, per Yokota and Adams
SU(3)_C identified across F_4 and Cl(6)/PS routes via common complex structure J on W
F_4 route provides gauge algebra only; Cl(6) route provides gauge algebra + chirality
U(1) matching via rank counting (rank 4 = rank F_4, minus SU(3) and SU(2) Cartans leaves 1)
Gap B steps 1 and 2 classified as structurally independent (Spin(9) contains G_2 acting transitively on S^6)
Synthesis theorem stated as conditional on 3 gaps (A, B1, B2), not unconditional
Generation structure and spectral action classified as LOW severity for this paper
Started milestone v6.0: Gap C -- Complexification from C*-Measurement Maps
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle -- closing Gap C in Paper 7 derivation chain

decisions.summary: Route 1 (Effros-Stormer) resolved NEGATIVE -- conditional expectations do not force V_{1/2} complexification
Peirce interface V_1 . V_{1/2} is trivially scalar multiplication (eigenvalue 1/2 action)
No C*-subalgebra inside h_3(O) -- observer is external (Shirshov-Cohn)
Route 2 (state-effect duality) NEGATIVE -- P_1(v*w) is real scalar; C-linear extension inert on V_1 = R
Compatible complex structures form 56-dim family O(16)/U(8), none selected by pairing
Route 3 (GNS) NEGATIVE -- three independent obstructions (exceptional, parity, bottleneck)
Route 4 (tensor product) WEAK POSITIVE -- generic algebraic tautology, not h_3(O)-specific
Hanche-Olsen JC-algebra framework bypassed (V_{1/2} is module, not algebra; h_3(O) not JC)

custom_conventions.sequential_product_notation: a & b (non-commutative, per vdW arXiv:1803.11139 Def. 2)
custom_conventions.orthogonality: a perp b iff a & b = 0
custom_conventions.corrected_product: a & b = sum lambda_i C_{p_i}(b) + sum sqrt(lambda_i lambda_j) P_{ij}(b)
custom_conventions.composite_product: (a tensor b) & (c tensor d) = (a & c) tensor (b & d)
